pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and setup_opentoonz.sh explicitly place the scene at this path.
setup_task.sh initializes and export_result.sh looks for output frames exactly in this path.
verifier.py checks for 'target_skin' explicitly using the RGB array [60, 60, 90].
verifier.py checks for 'target_shirt' explicitly using the RGB array [40, 30, 60].
verifier.py uses a tolerance distance of 30 when evaluating the target colors against rendered pixels.
verifier.py only checks if `file_count > 0` and `new_files >= 1` and evaluates a single analysis frame. It does not enforce a minimum of 10 frames.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the public 'dwanko_run.tnz' sample project from the OpenToonz github repository. The project must be exported to '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/night_scene/'. To pass programmatic verification, the rendered frames must exhibit the target skin color (RGB 60, 60, 90) and target shirt color (RGB 40, 30, 60) within a tolerance distance of 30. While the task metadata claims a minimum of 10 frames is required, the verifier only technically enforces the creation of at least 1 new output frame during the task session.
